rulings.Case Title: ERIN WIDEN VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Request For Attorneys Fees And Costs (Ccp 664.6)
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property / Housing motion calendar for May 11, 2026, line 1. Cross Complainants' Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement and Request for Attorneys Fees and Costs (CCP 664.6) is GRANTED. Attorney's fees granted as requested. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRICK & TIMBER, INC. VS. SONIA TRUDEAU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff To: 1) Compel Responses By Defendant To Form Interrogatories-Unlawful Detainer (Set One) Without Objection; Compel Responses By Defendant To Form Interrogatories-General (Set One); Compel Responses By Defendant To Special Interrogatories (Set One) Without Objection; Compel Responses By Defendant To Request For Production Of Documents (Set One) Without Objection; 2) Deem Admitted By Defendant Requests For Admission (Set One), And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property / Housing motion calendar for May 11, 2026, line 12. Plaintiff's Motion to: 1) Compel Responses by Defendant to Interrogatories and Request for Production Without Objection; 2) Deem Admitted by Defendant Request for Admission, and for Monetary Sanctions is OFF CALENDAR. This motion was continued and Plaintiff was ordered to provide courtesy copies clearly identifying where the Court may find the supporting memorandum and any other papers filed in support of this motion. Plaintiff provided courtesy copies with a cover letter indicating the supporting memorandum and declaration of counsel were combined into the single filing entitled "NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION . . ." which does not specify in the caption that these supporting papers are included. This combined filing does not comply with CRC 3.1112. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BWF FORGE TL PROPERTIES OWNER, LLC VS. RUBEN BECKER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property / Housing motion calendar for May 11, 2026, line 14. Defendants' Motion to Quash Service of Summons is DENIED. Defendants move to quash service of the unlawful detainer summons under Delta Imports Inc. v. Municipal Court (1983) 146 Cal.App.3d 1033, on the grounds that the Complaint fails to state a cause of action for unlawful detainer. However, Defendants' argument is based entirely on facts that do not appear on the face of the Complaint. Defendants have failed to identify any deficiency in the Complaint to support their conclusion that the Complaint fails to state a cause of action for unlawful detainer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
